feed.title: Christopher Alberto — Blog
feed.subtitle: Notes on WordPress engineering, WooCommerce builds, and shipping for the modern web.

entry.title: FlyntWP: My Go-To for Building Lightning-Fast, Bloat-Free WordPress Sites
entry.summary: I’ve spent years in the WordPress development trenches, and like many of you, I’ve wrestled with the promise and frustration of low-code page builders.

entry.title: Ditch the Drag-and-Drop: Why Custom WordPress Themes Reign Supreme
entry.summary: There’s no denying the immediate allure of a WordPress page builder.

entry.title: Decoupling WordPress: Building a Headless Frontend with Next.js and Automated CI/CD
entry.summary: We often think of WordPress as a monolithic platform, a backend and frontend tightly coupled, dictating how our content is presented.

entry.title: Reclaim Your WordPress: Why Custom Code Obliterates Bloated Builders
entry.summary: I’ve seen it countless times: a client needs a WordPress site, and the quickest path seems to be a drag-and-drop builder like Elementor or Divi.

entry.title: Ditch the Bloat: Why Vanilla WordPress, Custom Blocks, and `$wpdb` are Your Only Path to Enterprise-Grade Performance
entry.summary: Let's cut through the marketing fluff and get brutally honest about WordPress development.

entry.title: Architecting Headless WordPress: Next.js, REST, and Automated CI/CD with GitHub Actions
entry.summary: My journey into modern web development quickly led me to question the traditional coupled WordPress setup.

entry.title: Ditching Divi: Why I Code My WordPress Themes From Scratch
entry.summary: My journey with WordPress began, like many, with the seductive promise of visual drag-and-drop.

entry.title: Tired of Bloat? Why FlyntWP is My Go-To for Lean, Fast WordPress Sites
entry.summary: For years, I found myself in a constant battle with WordPress page builders.

entry.title: Building Better WordPress: How Sage Unlocks Clean, Maintainable Code
entry.summary: For years, the WordPress development landscape felt like a choose-your-own-adventure between spaghetti code and page builder bloat.

entry.title: Building WordPress Themes The Right Way: Why Sage Cured My Development Headaches
entry.summary: For years, WordPress theme development felt like a constant battle.

entry.title: Ditching the Drag-and-Drop: My Journey to Faster WordPress with FlyntWP
entry.summary: I've spent years building WordPress sites, and while page builders promised speed and ease, they often delivered bloat.

entry.title: Building Beyond the Builder: Why Custom WordPress Themes Win on Performance and Scale
entry.summary: For anyone diving into WordPress, the allure of page builders like Elementor or Divi is undeniable.

entry.title: From Bloatware to Blade: Why Sage is My WordPress Development Game-Changer
entry.summary: I've spent years navigating the WordPress ecosystem, and if there's one constant frustration I've encountered, it's the struggle between rapid development and…

entry.title: Building Better: Why I Chose FlyntWP for Lightning-Fast WordPress Development
entry.summary: I've built my fair share of WordPress sites over the years, and I’ve seen the rise and, frankly, the performance fall of many popular low-code page builders.

entry.title: Decoupling WordPress: Building a Headless Backend with CI/CD for Production
entry.summary: I've spent years pushing WordPress beyond its blog-centric origins, and lately, my focus has shifted dramatically towards leveraging it as a robust, headless…

entry.title: From Bloat to Brilliance: Unpacking the Power of Custom-Coded WordPress Themes
entry.summary: Let's talk WordPress.

entry.title: Why I Ditched Page Builders for Hand-Coded WordPress: The Performance & Scalability Edge
entry.summary: For years, like many of you, I dabbled extensively with WordPress page builders.

entry.title: Beyond the Block Editor: Architecting Headless WordPress with GitHub Actions
entry.summary: For years, WordPress has been the backbone of countless websites, renowned for its ease of use and vast plugin ecosystem.

entry.title: That Lighthouse Score and the Headless Rabbit Hole I Fell Down
entry.summary: I remember staring at the Lighthouse report for that client's old WordPress site last year.

entry.title: That Time I Rebuilt a Site After Its Page Builder Imploded
entry.summary: I remember this one project, maybe three years back now.
